mappings.rationale: Directly covers nuclear structure, nuclear reactions, fission, fusion, isotopes, and nuclear models that constitute the core topics of this section.
Directly covers elementary particles, their classification, fundamental forces, conservation laws, symmetry operations, and the Standard Model discussed in this section.
Covers nuclear fission chain reactions, nuclear bombs, and thermonuclear weapons (hydrogen bombs) discussed in the section's treatment of fission and fusion.
Directly relevant to controlled nuclear fission, fission chain reactions, critical mass, and the prospects for thermonuclear reactors discussed in this section.
Directly covers radioactive nuclei, types of radioactivity, decay laws, alpha/beta/gamma decay, and applications of radioactivity central to this section.
Directly relevant to the section's detailed coverage of isotopes, their classification, formation by nuclear reactions, separation methods, and applications.
Covers quantum field theory, renormalization, and the quantum mechanical framework underlying nuclear structure and elementary particle physics.
Relevant to relativistic mechanics and charge conjugation operations in the context of elementary particle interactions and mass-energy equivalence in nuclear reactions.
Covers the formation of elements through nuclear processes and nucleosynthesis, relevant to the section's discussion of nuclear reactions creating elements.
Covers the fundamental constituents of matter including nuclear and subatomic particles, directly relevant to this section's scope.
Covers the historical discovery of the neutron, nuclear fission, and the development of nuclear and particle physics concepts discussed in this section.
Relevant to the liquid drop model of the nucleus and the theoretical framework for understanding nuclear fission that Bohr contributed to.
Provides coverage of nuclear physics, elementary particles, and fundamental forces directly relevant to the topics in this section.
Relevant to the formation and evolution of atomic nuclei in the universe and thermonuclear reactions in the Sun and stars discussed in this section.
Relevant to thermonuclear reactions in the Sun and stars, and the nucleosynthesis processes that form atomic nuclei discussed in this section.
Directly relevant to thermonuclear fusion reactions in the Sun discussed in this section's coverage of nuclear fusion phenomena.
Explores the nature of the vacuum, antimatter, and fundamental particle concepts relevant to elementary particle physics in this section.
Covers nuclear power generation through controlled fission and the prospects for fusion power, topics discussed in the section's treatment of nuclear energy.
Covers foundational concepts in quantum field theory and symmetry principles relevant to the section's discussion of elementary particles and conservation laws.
Relevant to the section's extensive discussion of symmetry operations, conservation laws, and classification systems in elementary particle physics.
Covers stellar nucleosynthesis and thermonuclear processes relevant to the formation and evolution of atomic nuclei in the universe.
Relevant to nuclear magnetic resonance phenomena and nuclear magnetic moments discussed in the section's coverage of nuclear properties.
Relevant to isotopic variations of elements and the relationship between nuclear structure and elemental properties discussed in this section.
Provides context for fusion energy prospects and the basic conditions required for thermonuclear reactors discussed in this section.
